phase.name: Add loom research (verb + whiteboard-composed skill + recovery)

unit: cli/verbs/research.ts + 8 new event types + loom wiring + phase-2 substrate sync

contract.goal: Add the `loom research <slug-or-topic> --research-file=<path> --notes-file=<path> [--no-loom] [--no-commit] [--pretty]` CLI verb that writes RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md at project root, auto-adopts loom substrate by default (mirroring cli/verbs/plan.ts), and emits research-started + research-completed events when loom is present. Lands all 8 research-* event types (PLAN.md 3.3) in cli/lib/types.ts so D2's skill can emit the remaining 6 without re-extending the type system. Wires `research` as a verbless namespace in cli/loom.ts. D1 ships the CLI half of Phase 3 (sub-section 3.1) plus the type-level declaration of all 8 event names. Carries the phase-2-close manifest+events reconciliation that was stashed from the prior branch (substrate sync, not a behavioral change).
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/types.ts adds 8 new event-type aliases (ResearchStartedEvent, ResearchCompletedEvent, ResearchShiftEvent, ResearchPanelSpawnedEvent, ResearchPanelVerdictEvent, ResearchFactCheckSpawnedEvent, ResearchFactCheckVerdictEvent, ResearchBudgetExhaustedEvent) all wired into the discriminated `Event` union.

cli/verbs/research.ts exports `researchVerb` + `RESEARCH_VERBS = { research: researchVerb }`. Accepts <slug-or-topic> + --research-file + --notes-file + --no-loom + --no-commit + --pretty. Auto-adopts loom unless --no-loom or pre-existing manifest.json. Emits research-started before the commit and research-completed after the commit, when loom is present.
cli/loom.ts wires `research` as a verbless namespace (NAMESPACES + VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE + VERBLESS_NAMESPACES — same pattern as doctor/plan/revise-plan).

cli/verbs/research.test.ts covers happy path (files copied + loom adopted + commit fired + events appended with asserted detail shapes), --no-loom (no adopt + no events), pre-existing-manifest recovery (no adopt + events still emitted into pre-existing log), --no-commit (no git, events still emitted), --pretty, slug-from-topic derivation, full-slug passthrough, dir-exists-no-RESEARCH, committed-RESEARCH refuses with `research-exists-committed`, missing-positional + missing-flag + missing-source-file error paths, registry shape.
cli/loom.test.ts adds a dispatch test verifying `research` routes through the verbless-namespace dispatch (missing-args surfaces, not unknown-verb or missing-verb).
`npm test` green at 485 tests (was 468 after Phase 2; +17 = 16 in research.test.ts + 1 in loom.test.ts dispatch).

Smoke: `bin/loom research <topic> --research-file=… --notes-file=… --no-commit --pretty` against a scratch projects root: exit 0, writes RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md, auto-adopts loom substrate, emits project-initialized + research-started + research-completed in events.jsonl.

Substrate reconciliation: phase 2 manifest+events updates (phase 2 status `completed`, PR #2 state `merged`, phase-completed event recorded) land in this commit. These updates happened locally after the phase-2 branch was pushed and so didn't ship with PR #2 — they ride into main with the phase-3 PR.
rules_applied: CLAUDE.md: pure-pattern relocation of the plan.ts shape into research.ts — same args parsing, same loom-adopt branching, same git-runner seam. Differences are intentional and minimal: the second file pair (RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md instead of PLAN.md + INTERVIEW.md), the event emission gating, and the up-front source-file existence check (fail-fast before mkdir).
CLAUDE.md: don't add features beyond the task — the verb is intentionally narrow. The skill is D2's concern. The deferred `revise-research` verb is NOT implemented here.
PLAN.md Phase 3 sub-section 3.1 dictates the verb interface verbatim; sub-section 3.3 dictates the 8 event names verbatim.
LOOM-CONVENTIONS verbless-namespace pattern: `loom <namespace>` where the namespace IS the only verb (sibling: doctor, plan, revise-plan).

Three-phase pattern (CLAUDE.md): D1 grounds the CLI surface that D2's skill orchestrates. D2 will be able to invoke `bin/loom research` as a black box once its grill-me / domain-shift / fact-check / human-auto-duality flows are authored.
disqualifiers: Touching the `/loom-research` skill body — that's D2.
Emitting non-CLI events from the verb (the skill emits shift, panel, fact-check, budget-exhausted at the corresponding decision points; the verb only emits started + completed).
Adding a `revise-research` verb — explicitly deferred per PLAN.md.
Auto-adopting loom into projects that already have a manifest.json (recovery / coexistence path mirrors plan.ts exactly).
Changing cli/loom.ts dispatch logic — verbless-namespace flow was already wired in Phase 2.
inputs: projects/2026-05-17-loom-absorb-draft/PLAN.md:255-321 (Phase 3 spec; 3.1 = CLI verb interface; 3.3 = event names)
cli/lib/types.ts (Event discriminated-union shape, pre-change at lines 51-127)
cli/loom.ts (verbless-namespace dispatch wiring, pre-change at lines 47-50, 122-132)

actions: Synced post-merge: checkout main, pulled PR #2's changes, cut branch `phase-3-loom-research`. Phase-2-close manifest+events updates were stashed before checkout (they happened locally after the phase-2 push and so weren't in PR #2's snapshot) and popped onto phase-3-loom-research.

Reconciled phase 2 state on the new branch via `bin/loom phase update 2 --status=completed --branch=phase-2-move-plan-verbs-to-loom --pr=2 --url=… --pr-state=merged`. The verb fired a second `phase-completed` event (idempotency quirk — see notes_for_pr), but the manifest correctly reflects merged-PR state.

Extended cli/lib/types.ts with 8 new EventBase types (ResearchStartedEvent, ResearchCompletedEvent, ResearchShiftEvent, ResearchPanelSpawnedEvent, ResearchPanelVerdictEvent, ResearchFactCheckSpawnedEvent, ResearchFactCheckVerdictEvent, ResearchBudgetExhaustedEvent) and wired all 8 into the `Event` discriminated union. Added a section header comment explaining the D1/D2 split.

Authored cli/verbs/research.ts: researchVerb mirrors planVerb's structure (parseArgs → slug resolution → collision check → file writes → optional loom adopt → optional commit) with three intentional additions: (a) source-file existence checks before any mkdir/write (fail-fast on the common 'wrong path' mistake), (b) research-started emission after writes but before the commit, (c) research-completed emission after the commit, both gated on loom-presence (fresh-adopt OR pre-existing manifest.json). Trimmed an unused readFileSync import.

Authored cli/verbs/research.test.ts: 16 tests across happy-path, --no-loom, pre-existing manifest, --no-commit, --pretty, slug derivation, full-slug passthrough, dir-exists, recovery overwrite, committed-collision refuse, missing-arg paths, and missing-source paths. Event assertions check both event ordering (toEqual on the names array) and detail shape (research-started.detail.topic + research-completed.detail.research_path / notes_path).

Wired into cli/loom.ts: imported RESEARCH_VERBS; added `research` to NAMESPACES, VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE, VERBLESS_NAMESPACES. Updated the VERBLESS_NAMESPACES inline comment to enumerate the four verbless namespaces.
Added cli/loom.test.ts dispatch test for `dispatch: verbless namespace (research) routes to researchVerb (missing-args surfaces)`, modeled on the existing plan/revise-plan tests.
Ran `npm test`: 34 test files passed (was 33; +1 from research.test.ts), 485 tests passed (was 468; +17), 0 failures, ~650ms.

Smoke-tested `bin/loom research smoke-research-topic --research-file=… --notes-file=… --no-commit --pretty` against a scratch projects root: exit 0, writes RESEARCH.md + RESEARCH-NOTES.md, auto-adopts loom substrate (manifest.json, config.json, events.jsonl, checkins/, sessions/), emits project-initialized + research-started + research-completed events.

Derived evaluator panel via `bin/guild derive-panel --files=cli/verbs/research.ts,cli/verbs/research.test.ts,cli/lib/types.ts,cli/loom.ts,cli/loom.test.ts` → evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming (fallback rules; PANEL-COMPOSITION.md still unreadable from marketplace cwd).
Invoked /guild-validate with the four evaluators. All four returned `approved`. Aggregated verdict: approved, 0 blocking findings, 0 advisory findings, 0 conflicts.

changes_since_previous: First unit on this branch. Phase 2 closed on the prior branch (merged via PR #2). The phase-2 close substrate updates (manifest status `completed`, PR #2 state `merged`, phase-completed event) ride into main with this PR — they were applied locally after the phase-2 push and so didn't ship with PR #2's snapshot.
notes_for_pr: The `loom research` verb is the first loom CLI handler to emit domain events (research-started, research-completed) from its body, beyond the standard substrate vocabulary (project-initialized, phase-*, checkin-created, pr-*, session-saved, retro-written, archived, note). PLAN.md 3.3 explicitly dictates this. Pattern: emit started after file writes but before the commit; emit completed after the commit succeeds. On commit failure the verb returns with an error and completed never fires, leaving a started without a matching completion in events.jsonl — that's the correct partial-failure signal for forensics.

Event emission is gated on loom-presence: either (a) the verb auto-adopted loom this run, or (b) a manifest.json already existed (the recovery/coexistence path). The --no-loom + no-pre-existing case is the only one that skips emission — there's no events.jsonl to write into.

Contract phrasing nit (caught by the contract-fit evaluator): the contract said `started before file write` but the code emits started AFTER the writes. The phrasing should have read `started before the commit, completed after the commit` — the writes happen first because, in the fresh-adopt path, the events.jsonl doesn't exist yet until writeLoomSubstrate runs. Comment at cli/verbs/research.ts:212-217 documents this gating. Evaluator approved with the refinement noted.
The `research-exists-committed` error message points users at the Deferred `loom revise-research` verb (PLAN.md `**Deferred:**` section). No deprecation shim; just a clean signpost.

8 event types declared in D1; only 2 are emitted by the CLI verb. The other 6 (research-shift, research-panel-spawned, research-panel-verdict, research-fact-check-spawned, research-fact-check-verdict, research-budget-exhausted) are declared here so D2's skill code can emit them via the existing appendEvent helper without re-extending the type union.

Phase-2-close substrate sync rides into this PR: phase 2 PR #2 was merged before the local manifest+events post-close updates were pushed, so they were stashed off phase-2-branch and re-applied on phase-3-branch. The current state on origin/main has phase 2 in `in-progress`; this PR flips it to `completed` with PR state `merged`. A future `bin/loom pr reconcile` verb (mentioned in the router rules) would automate this kind of out-of-band sync.

Calling `bin/loom phase update --status=completed` on an already-completed phase re-fires the `phase-completed` event in events.jsonl. Minor substrate idempotency quirk — events.jsonl is append-only by design, but the verb could check current state and skip the event emission when no transition occurred. Not blocking this PR; flagged for a future substrate hardening pass.

Same fallback-panel observation as Phase 1 + Phase 2: `bin/guild derive-panel` runs in fallback mode from the marketplace cwd because `.claude/agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md` isn't symlinked there (the spec lives at `agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md`). Not in scope; flagged once more for a future cross-cutting workstream.

ResearchShiftEvent's name breaks the `<subject>-<verb-past>` cadence of its siblings (`shift` is a noun, not a past-tense verb). The naming evaluator flagged this as a soft observation; the design choice was deliberate — `shift` IS the event (a discrete moment in the research arc, not an action taken on it). The string literal `'research-shift'` and the type name `ResearchShiftEvent` parallel cleanly, which is what the naming convention is ultimately preserving.
